graph.confusable_reason: Both produce late-season interveinal chlorosis and necrosis with green veins on soybean foliage, so leaves alone do not separate them.
graph.shared_presentation: Interveinal chlorosis and necrosis between green veins on upper leaves during the reproductive stages.

forks.question: What is the growth stage and field history when symptoms appear?
forks.a_signal: Foliar symptoms from early-to-mid reproductive (R3-R4), in fields with SDS history, early planting into cool/wet soils, or SCN issues.
forks.b_signal: Symptoms mainly mid-to-late reproductive (R5-R7) in cooler, moist seasons, with a history of brown stem rot.

forks.question: How dramatic is the foliar collapse and do petioles stay attached as bare skeletons?
forks.a_signal: Rapid defoliation with petioles remaining attached to the stem (bare-petiole skeletons).
forks.b_signal: Less dramatic yellowing/necrosis with fewer bare-petiole skeletons.

forks.question: Split the lower stem and examine the pith versus the outer vascular tissue.
forks.a_signal: Pith stays white while the outer vascular tissue/cortex is brown to gray.
forks.b_signal: Pith is brown, often continuous and cardboard-like, through the internodes.

forks.question: Examine roots and crown for rot and fungal growth.
forks.a_signal: Roots rotted, sometimes with bluish fungal growth or blue spore masses near the crown/taproot.
forks.b_signal: Roots relatively sound compared with the severe stem pith browning.

graph.narrative_a: Because foliar interveinal chlorosis plus a white pith with brown outer vascular tissue and root rot bearing bluish growth match SDS, I classify this as Sudden Death Syndrome rather than Brown Stem Rot.
graph.narrative_b: Because foliar symptoms plus a continuous brown, cardboard-like pith with relatively healthy roots match BSR, I classify this as Brown Stem Rot rather than Sudden Death Syndrome.
graph.management_a: No effective in-season rescue; manage with SDS-tolerant varieties, improved drainage, SCN control, and avoiding early planting into cold, wet soils.
graph.management_b: Manage with resistant varieties and rotation away from soybean; foliar fungicides do not control brown stem rot.
